metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists after 39 days in Qualified. Richard Nicholls expressed interest in iRisk and iSupplier modules per the 25 May Next Step, but no cost baseline, savings target, cycle-time reduction, error rate, or headcount impact has been articulated in any Salesforce field or discovered Avoma call. The Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c fields are all empty. Without a quantified anchor a $150k S2P investment cannot be justified to the CFO or CPO, and the deal will stall in Qualified or lose to a lower-cost alternative or do-nothing. The immediate move is to run a discovery session with Richard Nicholls and the CPO to quantify current-state pain (manual PO cycle time, supplier onboarding delays, maverick spend leakage, contract compliance gaps) and map those to iRisk/iSupplier/Merlin ROI, targeting a specific dollar or percentage savings figure to carry into the on-site demo.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 25 May, 10 Jun, 30 Jun (no champion behaviors)
Shannon Robartson outreach 30 Jun, no reply yet
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified after 39 days. Richard Nicholls is an evaluator (Group Procurement Manager, contact role Evaluator) with no champion behaviors: he scheduled the initial meeting with the relevant stakeholder on 25 May and expressed interest, but there is no evidence of advocacy (introducing Zycus to the CPO or CFO, pushing back on competitors, driving the internal agenda, or providing insider guidance on the decision process or politics). The unidentified CPO went on leave and the on-site meeting was postponed with no proactive reschedule from the buyer side, suggesting passive engagement. Shannon Robartson was contacted by the rep on 30 Jun but her role, level, and willingness to champion are unknown (no reply recorded, no prior Salesforce history). The X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c and Champion_Identified__c fields are empty. Without a buyer-side champion who can navigate internal politics, map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, and advocate for Zycus over competitors or do-nothing, this deal cannot progress past Qualified. The immediate move is to test Shannon Robartson's willingness and access in the next 7 days (if she replies to the 30 Jun outreach), and separately to ask Richard Nicholls in the rescheduled on-site meeting to identify someone in procurement or the CPO's office who is excited about the iRisk/Merlin/ANA capabilities and willing to sponsor the business case internally, then invest in building that person as a champion through value-add follow-up and insider coaching.

sources: No competitor references in Next Step or tasks
competition.narrative: Competition is completely unknown after 39 days. The Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, and Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c fields are all empty. No Avoma call was discovered, no Next Step or task references a competitor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua), and no competitive discussion has been recorded. It is unknown whether Monadelphous is running a multi-vendor evaluation in parallel, has an incumbent S2P system they are replacing, or is approaching this as a greenfield sole-source opportunity. The risk is that a competitor is already in the frame, running discovery with the CPO and CFO, positioning against Zycus, and the deal team is blind to the competitive wedge and the buyer's shortlist. The immediate move is to ask Richard Nicholls and the CPO in the rescheduled on-site meeting who else they are looking at, what current systems or processes they use today for sourcing/supplier management/risk/contract management, and what alternatives (vendor or do-nothing) they are comparing Zycus against, then position the Merlin AI negotiation agent and ANA spend analytics as differentiation against traditional S2P incumbents.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 25 May (interest in iRisk, iSupplier, no pain quantified)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is unquantified and not anchored to a named owner. Richard Nicholls expressed interest in iRisk and iSupplier modules on 25 May per Next Step, implying concern with supplier risk management and supplier collaboration/onboarding, but no specific operational or financial pain was articulated: no manual cycle time, no supplier onboarding delay, no contract compliance failure rate, no maverick spend leakage percentage. The Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c fields are all empty. No Avoma call was discovered to capture the buyer's voice. Without a confirmed pain owner (the CPO or a functional lead in procurement, risk, or finance) and a quantified pain statement, the deal has no urgency and cannot justify the $150k investment. The immediate move is to use the rescheduled on-site meeting to ask Richard Nicholls and the CPO to describe the top 2-3 procurement pain points they face today (e.g. slow supplier onboarding blocking project timelines, contract compliance gaps exposing the company to risk, lack of spend visibility preventing cost-reduction initiatives), quantify the impact in dollars or days, and confirm who owns each pain point internally.

paper_process.narrative: The paper process is completely unknown. No MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec review, or ERP integration scoping has been discussed in any Salesforce field, Next Step, task, or Avoma call (none discovered). The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, Merlin, iRisk, ANA) is a full S2P suite and will require ERP integration (spend data feeds, supplier master sync, invoice matching), yet no ERP system (SAP, Oracle, Workday, other) has been identified, no IT lead engaged, and no integration architecture discussion recorded. The Current_Contract_Expiration__c field is empty, so there is no forcing function from an incumbent contract end date. Monadelphous is a large ASX-listed mining services company, so legal, InfoSec, and procurement governance will be rigorous, but none of those workstreams has surfaced yet. The immediate move is to ask the CPO and Richard Nicholls in the on-site meeting to confirm the ERP system, introduce the IT/integration lead, and map the approval path for MSA/SOW, InfoSec review, and contract execution so the deal owner can front-run those gates rather than discovering them late.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is unengaged after 39 days. The likely EB is CFO Philip Trueman (from account org chart or public records), but he has not appeared in any Salesforce contact role, task, event, or Next Step entry, and no Avoma call was discovered. The X10a_Sponsor__c and Who_will_approve_budget__c fields are empty. A $150k S2P investment in mining services procurement will require CFO sign-off, yet the deal is single-threaded through Group Procurement Manager Richard Nicholls, an evaluator with no budget authority. The unidentified CPO (referenced in the 10 Jun Next Step as the main contact who went on leave) is likely the Decision Maker but not the Economic Buyer. Without EB engagement the deal cannot progress past Qualified and will stall indefinitely or lose to a competitor who maps the power base first. The immediate move is to ask Richard Nicholls or the CPO to introduce the deal owner or manager to Philip Trueman for a 20-minute value-alignment call anchored to procurement cost-reduction and contract-risk mitigation, positioning it as a strategic CFO-level initiative rather than an IT procurement project.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 10 Jun, 30 Jun (CPO, Richard Nicholls, Shannon Robartson)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible but incomplete. The Next Step entries show a three-actor structure: Richard Nicholls (Group Procurement Manager, evaluator-level), an unidentified CPO (main contact per 10 Jun, Decision Maker level but passive), and Shannon Robartson (unknown role, rep reached out 30 Jun). The Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c fields are empty, so the formal approval chain is unconfirmed. A $150k S2P investment in a mining services company will likely require CFO (Philip Trueman) and possibly MD (Zoran Bebic) sign-off, but neither has been engaged. The on-site meeting scheduled for 10 Jun was postponed when the CPO went on leave, suggesting the CPO is the gate for the next stage but the timeline is buyer-controlled and non-urgent. No Avoma call was discovered to reveal the buyer's internal process, escalation triggers, or committee structure. The immediate move is to confirm the approval chain and decision timeline in the rescheduled on-site meeting, asking explicitly who beyond the CPO and Richard Nicholls must sign off, what governance or procurement policy applies, and what milestones (shortlist, proposal, contract review) sit between now and Dec close.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 25 May (interest stated, no criteria)
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are unknown after 39 days. The Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c fields are empty, and no RFP, RFI, BRD, or evaluation scorecard has been referenced in any Next Step, task, or Avoma call (none discovered). Richard Nicholls expressed interest in iRisk and iSupplier modules on 25 May but did not articulate the evaluation framework, weighting, or must-have vs nice-to-have criteria. The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, iRequest, Merlin, iRisk, ANA) is broad, suggesting a full S2P transformation, but it is unknown whether Monadelphous will run a formal RFP, a vendor shortlist comparison, or a sole-source evaluation, and what technical, commercial, or functional gates the solution must clear. The immediate move is to use the rescheduled on-site meeting to ask the CPO and Richard Nicholls to walk through their evaluation process, decision timeline, scorecard criteria, and any formal procurement governance requirements, then map Zycus strengths (Merlin AI-driven negotiation, iRisk contract compliance, ANA spend analytics) to their top-weighted criteria.

north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified-stage deal 23 days from initial engagement, 151 days to close. Stalled for 16 days awaiting CPO return and on-site reschedule. No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker engaged, single-threaded through evaluator Richard Nicholls, no champion, no quantified value case, no competitive intel, and 25% owner confidence. Rep outreach to Shannon Robartson 30 Jun represents a pivot attempt but no buyer reply yet. Without power-base mapping, multi-threading, and a confirmed on-site in the next 14 days this deal will drift in Qualified indefinitely.

flags: Re-engage buyer immediately to reschedule postponed on-site meeting (16 days elapsed since 10 Jun CPO personal-leave postponement; no confirmed new date). This is the forcing function to restart momentum, map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, capture the BRD and evaluation criteria, surface competitive intelligence, and convert Richard Nicholls from evaluator to coach. Without the on-site meeting, the deal cannot progress from Qualified to Shortlisted, and the 25% confidence will not improve. Act within 7 days to prevent further drift.

Secure Economic Buyer and Decision Maker access during rescheduled on-site meeting. Richard Nicholls (Group Procurement Manager, Evaluator) is the single contact; no CFO, MD, or CPO engagement documented. For a $150k S2P suite, budget authority sits with the relevant stakeholder (CFO) or Zoran Bebic (MD), both on the account but not on the opportunity. The unidentified CPO mentioned 10 Jun may be the Decision Maker. Use the on-site meeting to ask Richard Nicholls for an introduction to the budget holder and the CPO, framing the ask around validating the business case and ROI model. Without EB/DM access, commercials and negotiation cannot begin, and forecast defensibility stays low.

Build a champion with power access and activate AI/Merlin wedge. Richard Nicholls is evaluator-level with positive sentiment but no advocacy evidence (no EB introduction, no internal selling, no competitive push-back on Zycus's behalf). Convert him to a coach by asking for his candid read on the internal process, competition, and political landscape during the on-site meeting. Then identify a second, more senior champion (the CPO, a Productivity & Innovation lead like Lisa Yewers on the account, or a finance lead) who owns the pain (procurement cost, supplier risk, working capital efficiency) and can sponsor Zycus to the CFO/MD. The Merlin AI negotiation agent is in scope but not yet positioned as a wedge—use the on-site to demo Merlin's supplier price history negotiation capability and tie it to Monadelphous's contractor procurement pain (volatile supplier pricing, manual negotiation cycles). A champion who sees Merlin as a competitive advantage will drive urgency and isolate Coupa/Ariba as commoditized alternatives.

Re-engage buyer immediately to reschedule postponed on-site meeting (16 days elapsed since 10 Jun CPO personal-leave postponement; no confirmed new date). This is the forcing function to restart momentum, map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, capture the BRD and evaluation criteria, surface competitive intelligence, and convert Richard Nicholls from evaluator to coach. Without the on-site meeting, the deal cannot progress from Qualified to Shortlisted, and the 25% confidence will not improve. Act within 7 days to prevent further drift.

Secure Economic Buyer and Decision Maker access during rescheduled on-site meeting. Richard Nicholls (Group Procurement Manager, Evaluator) is the single contact; no CFO, MD, or CPO engagement documented. For a $150k S2P suite, budget authority sits with the relevant stakeholder (CFO) or Zoran Bebic (MD), both on the account but not on the opportunity. The unidentified CPO mentioned 10 Jun may be the Decision Maker. Use the on-site meeting to ask Richard Nicholls for an introduction to the budget holder and the CPO, framing the ask around validating the business case and ROI model. Without EB/DM access, commercials and negotiation cannot begin, and forecast defensibility stays low.

Build a champion with power access and activate AI/Merlin wedge. Richard Nicholls is evaluator-level with positive sentiment but no advocacy evidence (no EB introduction, no internal selling, no competitive push-back on Zycus's behalf). Convert him to a coach by asking for his candid read on the internal process, competition, and political landscape during the on-site meeting. Then identify a second, more senior champion (the CPO, a Productivity & Innovation lead like Lisa Yewers on the account, or a finance lead) who owns the pain (procurement cost, supplier risk, working capital efficiency) and can sponsor Zycus to the CFO/MD. The Merlin AI negotiation agent is in scope but not yet positioned as a wedge—use the on-site to demo Merlin's supplier price history negotiation capability and tie it to Monadelphous's contractor procurement pain (volatile supplier pricing, manual negotiation cycles). A champion who sees Merlin as a competitive advantage will drive urgency and isolate Coupa/Ariba as commoditized alternatives.
